HERO_DATA.description: A technically sharp game developer building immersive, feature-rich games across PC, Mobile, VR, and Web. Targeting Senior / Lead Unity Developer roles in multiplayer, VR, or systems architecture. Passionate about elegant systems, real-time multiplayer, and shipping polished products.

bullets: Architected the full technical foundation of a VR MMO shipped on Meta Quest — networking, backend APIs, AI, and 15+ synchronized gameplay systems
Led Unity DOTS adoption across the team; converted world streaming and AI systems to ECS for scalable multiplayer performance
Designed and implemented matchmaking, party, and friends systems powering solo and co-op multiplayer across WebGL and VR
Contributed to the development and customization of 6+ serious games for cybersecurity training, adapting gameplay systems and content for multiple enterprise clients.
Architected and delivered multi-platform Unity solutions across WebGL, VR, Kinect, and mobile, ensuring consistent performance and user experience.
Built core gameplay systems (JSON-driven content, game flow, validation logic) while mentoring junior developers and improving code quality and team productivity.
Delivered core gameplay systems for a large-scale HGV training simulator, including AI traffic, vehicle systems, camera architecture, and training logic.
Designed and implemented structured training scenarios with validation, failure conditions, and LMS-integrated performance tracking for enterprise deployment.
Developed 10 Tawla (iOS/Android backgammon) — built ELO ranking, matchmaking, friend system, and adaptive AI using Nakama Server
Shipped and maintained the game post-launch on iOS and Android, iterating on features and performance based on player feedback
Co-founded the studio and led all technical development across projects spanning PC, Mobile, and VR over 2.5 years
Architected networked multiplayer for Sky Striders (PC) using Photon Bolt — owning movement sync, abilities, match state, and disconnect handling end-to-end
Shipped Autoliv Explore 3D (iOS AR) — Unity Case Study featured on unity.com — and That Just Happened (VR, Unreal Engine)
Co-founded and led development of desktop business management solutions for Egyptian clients, including a restaurant management system built in Java / SQL
Built AR menu visualization app using Unity and Vuforia, enabling customers to preview menu items in 3D

degrees.detail: 9-month intensive game development program with focus on game programming, VR development, Unity3D, design patterns, and game optimization.
Grade: Very Good.Graduation Project: Land of Puzzle — an interactive puzzle experience.

CONTACT_DATA.headline: Open to New
CONTACT_DATA.description: Primarily targeting Senior / Lead Unity Developer roles in multiplayer,PC,Mobile, VR, or systems architecture — open to full-time and contract engagements. Whether you're building a multiplayer game, a VR experience, or a mobile title, let's create something great together.

PROJECTS.name: HighStreet Calamity VR
PROJECTS.shortDesc: Highstreet: Calamity - VR Roguelike Arena Combat in a Manic Metaverse. Protect the FOMO DUCKS! Highstreet: Calamity is a fast-paced, class-based VR roguelike brawler set in Solera, a chaotic world where talking ducks run businesses, pelicans have strong opinions, and monster portals are "just an average Tuesday."
PROJECTS.desc: Highstreet: Calamity is a fast-paced, class-based VR roguelike brawler set in Solera, a chaotic world where talking ducks run businesses, pelicans have strong opinions, and monster portals are "just an average Tuesday." Players choose from unique classes with distinct abilities to battle waves of unpredictable enemies and bosses. Progression includes gear upgrades and branching skill paths. The game supports solo or co-op multiplayer with dynamic server allocation using GameLift. Social features include a lobby to connect with friends and enjoy the mayhem together.
features: Class-based combat system with unique abilities and playstyles
Procedurally generated arenas with dynamic hazards
Challenging monsters and bosses with distinct behaviors
Solo and co-op multiplayer modes with matchmaking
Progression system with gear upgrades and skill trees
Social hub for players to connect and show off achievements

myWork.title: DOTS Adoption & Team Enablement
myWork.description: Initiated and led the adoption of Unity DOTS and Netcode for Entities, establishing a scalable foundation for future MMO development and successfully onboarding the team through prototypes and knowledge sharing.

myWork.title: Technical Architecture & System Design
myWork.description: Architected the entire project technical foundation including authentication flow, backend APIs, database schema, networking, server infrastructure, and all networked game core systems: spawn, health/mana/stats, combat, abilities, skills, consumables, monsters, monster AI, aggro system, AI coordinator, and interaction system — all synchronized over the network.

myWork.title: Architecture Documentation Pipeline
myWork.description: Improved development efficiency and maintainability by building a dynamic architecture documentation system using Draw.io and Lucidchart integrated with Excel, enabling automatic diagram updates and clear system visualization.

myWork.title: Data-Driven Gameplay Pipeline
myWork.description: Accelerated design iteration by creating a fully data-driven pipeline that exposed gameplay configuration via Excel, allowing designers to modify abilities, stats, and AI without engineering dependency.

myWork.title: Multiplayer & Social Systems
myWork.description: Delivered core multiplayer features by implementing matchmaking, party, and friends systems, enabling seamless player connectivity and social gameplay experiences across servers.

myWork.title: AI System & Behavior Tools
myWork.description: Enabled scalable AI development by architecting and implementing a DOTS-based Behavior Tree system with custom editor tools, allowing rapid creation and extension of complex enemy behaviors.

myWork.title: DOTS Gameplay Systems Implementation
myWork.description: Delivered high-performance ECS gameplay systems using Unity DOTS — authoring, components, systems, and network sync workflows — all optimized for cache efficiency and scalable entity counts across live multiplayer sessions.

myWork.title: DOTS World Streaming System
myWork.description: Converted the world streaming system from MonoBehaviour to DOTS, enabling chunk-based streaming of entity-rendered art across max 9 chunks around the player, significantly improving runtime performance and scalability for large multiplayer arenas.

media.alt: Player Entity The Player has a LocalPlayerTagAuthoring script that bakes it into a DOTS entity. The streaming system queries this tag to track the player's position and decide which chunks to load or unload.

Grid Layer Manager The core controller of the system. It defines a 5x5 grid of 18x18 unit chunks covering 90x90 units total. It manages buffers for all chunks, currently loaded chunks, and pending load/unload requests.
Scene Hierarchy — Before Setup Before setup, the SubScene only contains the GridLayerManager and the Player. Nothing else exists yet.
Hierarchy After Setup Chunks Clicking Setup Chunks auto-generates 25 child GameObjects, one per grid cell. Each is an independent SubScene that can stream in and out of memory on its own.
Assets Attached to Chunks After running Attach Assets, world objects are placed inside the correct chunk based on their position. At runtime they appear and disappear with their parent chunk.
Runtime — As Player move chunks near the player are loaded. The rest of the world is completely unloaded and empty.
Runtime — As Player move chunks near the player are loaded. The rest of the world is completely unloaded and empty.
Runtime — As Player move chunks near the player are loaded. The rest of the world is completely unloaded and empty.
Runtime — As Player move chunks near the player are loaded. The rest of the world is completely unloaded and empty.
Runtime — As Player move chunks near the player are loaded. The rest of the world is completely unloaded and empty.

myWork.title: MonoBehaviour Gameplay Systems
myWork.description: Improved player onboarding and usability by implementing NPC Dialogue and Tooltip guidance systems using MonoBehaviour workflows, leveraging their flexibility for UI-driven and event-based interactions.

myWork.title: Server Infrastructure Migration
myWork.description: Ensured long-term scalability by leading the migration from Unity Multiplay to Amazon GameLift, selecting and integrating a more robust server hosting solution.

myWork.title: Automation & Deployment Tools [GameLift Fleet Management] [Vibe Coding]
myWork.description: Increased deployment efficiency by developing Python CLI automation tools for server build uploads and fleet creation with predefined configurations, significantly reducing manual setup time.

myWork.title: Player Data Optimization
myWork.description: Reduced backend load and improved performance by refactoring player avatar data handling and implementing synchronized local data wrappers across multiplayer systems.

myWork.title: Stability & Support
myWork.description: Maintained project stability and delivery quality by resolving critical bugs and continuously improving systems across gameplay and backend integration.

PROJECTS.name: Highstreet Campus
PROJECTS.shortDesc: Highstreet Campus was built as the company's metaverse entry point: a large-scale WebGL social hub with MMO-oriented networking, NFT commerce, and connected world travel into Highstreet Homes.
PROJECTS.desc: Highstreet Campus started as a social metaverse hub and strategic entry point for the company, with an early goal of building networking and infrastructure that could scale toward MMO requirements. The world combined social interaction spaces with NFT purchasing experiences, then expanded to include travel pods linking players to their homes in the previous Highstreet Homes project. The campus later introduced a VIP rooftop section with an outdoor garden, additional social activities, and a salon shop. One notable multiplayer challenge was a dynamic lab feature where players could switch lab interiors from an in-world console, requiring synchronized environment swaps while correctly handling other users already inside the affected area.
features: Metaverse entry point designed as a scalable social MMO hub
NFT shopping and social activity spaces across the campus
Travel pods connecting users from Campus to Highstreet Homes
VIP rooftop area with outdoor garden and social spaces
Salon shop and interactive interior spaces inside the campus
Dynamic lab system where players can swap lab interiors from an in-world console
Real-time synchronized interactions for shared systems like elevators and seats
Dynamic environment changes based on in-game events

myWork.title: Network System Enhancement & Channel Architecture
myWork.description: Improved the existing custom networking system by designing and implementing channel-based communication, enabling segmented data synchronization. This allowed players within the same area (e.g., campus, homes, external zones) to sync efficiently while reducing unnecessary network traffic.

myWork.title: Highstreet Homes Networking Migration
myWork.description: Replaced Photon networking in the already integrated Highstreet Homes system with the custom networking solution after introducing channel-based architecture. Unified both Campus and Homes under a single networking stack, enabling consistent synchronization of player customization,home customization, pets, and interactions while simplifying cross-system communication and reducing technical overhead.

myWork.title: Pet System Synchronization & UI Improvements
myWork.description: Enhanced pet systems by fixing synchronization across clients, improving spawning logic, and refining pet customization and UI to ensure consistent multiplayer behavior.

myWork.title: Dependency Injection Architecture
myWork.description: Improved codebase maintainability and modularity by introducing dependency injection using VContainer, managing core data such as player data and home data across systems.

myWork.title: Stability, Bug Fixing
myWork.description: Improved overall game stability and multiplayer reliability by resolving critical issues including crashes caused by null remote avatars, game freezes during loading, and RV-related crashes. Fixed synchronization inconsistencies in network behaviours, addressed totem and token data issues, corrected local name tag display, and reduced runtime warnings to ensure a smoother and more stable player experience.

PROJECTS.name: HGV Driver Training Simulator
PROJECTS.shortDesc: Interactive PC-based HGV driver training simulations focused on safety, risk awareness, and real-world industrial scenarios.
PROJECTS.desc: HGV Driver Training Software Solutions is a professional-grade simulation platform designed to improve driver safety and operational awareness in high-risk industrial environments. Built as an interactive PC training experience, the system places drivers into realistic workplace scenarios where they must identify hazards, follow correct procedures, and respond to critical situations. The training emphasizes experiential learning through immersive simulations that replicate real-world conditions, helping organizations reduce accidents and reinforce best practices. The solution supports structured learning flows, performance tracking, and integration with enterprise training systems, enabling scalable deployment across teams while ensuring measurable safety outcomes.
features: Realistic HGV driving and workplace safety simulations
Scenario-based hazard identification and response training
Interactive learning with guided and assessment-driven flows
Performance tracking and analytics for trainees
Enterprise-ready deployment and LMS compatibility

myWork.title: Training Simulation & Scenario Systems

myWork.title: Pause System Architecture
myWork.description: Eliminated pause-state bugs across all systems by designing an interface-based pause architecture with runtime object discovery, ensuring every gameplay system responds consistently to pause and resume.

myWork.title: Performance Optimization & Stability
myWork.description: Improved performance and stability through object pooling, optimized spawning systems, bug fixing, and resolving critical gameplay issues affecting AI behavior, physics, UI flow, and player progression.

PROJECTS.name: Autoliv Explore 3D
PROJECTS.shortDesc: An AR-powered sales tool that showcases automotive safety systems through interactive real-time 3D presentations.
PROJECTS.desc: Autoliv Explore 3D is an augmented reality and real-time 3D product visualization application designed to transform how automotive safety systems are presented and understood. Built for iOS using Unity and Vuforia, the app enables sales teams to showcase complex components such as airbags and seatbelts through interactive, immersive experiences. Instead of relying on static presentations or physical prototypes, users can place and inspect products in 3D, explore how they integrate inside vehicle environments, and communicate their functionality with clarity and impact. The experience focuses on smooth presentation flow, intuitive interaction, and strong visual storytelling, turning highly technical safety hardware into accessible, engaging demonstrations for global sales, marketing, and training teams.
features: Augmented reality product visualization
Interactive 3D exploration of automotive safety systems
Product placement and inspection in real-world environments
Exploded and contextual product views
Smooth guided presentation flow for sales demos
Optimized mobile performance for iPad devices

product Data ScriptableObject showing structured data for product presentation configuration, including references to other ScriptableObjects for callouts, pulse markers, placement indicators, viewport models, and tooltips
PulseMarkerData ScriptableObject defining data for pulse marker configuration

callout label Data ScriptableObject defining data for 3D callout labels, including text content and positioning

myWork.title: Custom Unity Timeline Track Suite
myWork.description: Engineered 11 custom Unity Timeline tracks from scratch, giving the team full keyframe control over systems not natively supported by Timeline. Tracks covered: GameEvents, standard and fade materials, Cinemachine camera settings, MeshRenderer visibility, TextMeshPro, screen transitions, highlight and outline effects (MagicOutline, RIMHighlight), dissolve effects (BeautifulDissolves, AdvancedDissolve), gradient backgrounds, realistic VFX, and real-time cross-section cuts. These tracks were used to build all cinematic product presentation sequences in the app.

media.alt: Custom Timeline Tracks in Unity Editor showing various track types such as GameEventTrack, MaterialTrack, CinemachineTrack, MeshRendererTrack, TextMeshProTrack, ScreenTransitionTrack, HighlightTrack, DissolveTrack, GradientBackgroundTrack, VFXTrack, and CrossSectionCutTrack

myWork.title: Car Interaction System

myWork.title: 3D Product Visualization Features
myWork.description: Shipped the complete product visualization feature set: swipe-driven navigation, overhead camera with pulse markers, depth-of-field focus control, and 3D callout labels that persist correctly inside the close-up inspection viewport.

myWork.title: Timeline Duplicate Editor Tool
myWork.description: Cut per-product presentation setup time significantly by building a custom Editor tool that duplicates Timeline assets along with all their track bindings — eliminating the most repetitive part of creating new product sequences.

PROJECTS.name: DOTS Behaviour Tree
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A high-performance Behaviour Tree framework for Unity DOTS/ECS with a visual editor, runtime inspector, interrupts, and extensible custom node templates.
PROJECTS.desc:
Dots Behaviour Tree is a Behaviour Tree framework built for Unity DOTS (Data-Oriented Technology Stack) on top of ECS architecture. It focuses on high-performance AI decision-making by baking authoring nodes into ECS entities and executing them through ECS systems. The framework includes a visual editor for graph-based authoring, a runtime inspector for live debugging, rich composite nodes, condition combinators, and interrupt support for high-priority behaviour preemption. It also provides script templates to accelerate creation of custom Action, Condition, Composite, and Interrupt nodes.

Requirements from the README include Unity 6000.0.23f1+ with Entities 1.3.2+ (Burst compatible), and the demo scene runs with URP 17.0.4+.
features: ECS-native behaviour tree execution with nodes baked into entities
Visual node-graph editor for authoring trees in Unity
Runtime inspector to watch node status transitions live during play mode
Built-in composite nodes: Sequencer, Selector, Parallel, Random, and Repeater
Condition combinators: AND, OR, and NOT for flexible decision gating
Interrupt system for high-priority preemption and control handoff
Script templates for custom Action, Condition, Composite, and Interrupt nodes

myWork.title: ECS-Native Behaviour Tree Runtime
myWork.description: Implemented a DOTS-first execution model where authored tree nodes are baked into ECS entities and updated by ECS systems for scalable runtime performance.

myWork.title: Visual Authoring + Runtime Inspector Tooling
myWork.description: Built editor tooling for graph-based tree authoring and runtime state inspection, enabling faster iteration and easier debugging of node state transitions.

myWork.title: Extensible Node, Condition, and Interrupt Architecture
myWork.description: Designed an extensible framework with templates and base classes for custom Action/Condition/Interrupt nodes, including condition combinators and interrupt preemption flows.

PROJECTS.name: Scriptable System
PROJECTS.shortDesc: [Unity Asset Store] A workflow package for event-driven gameplay with ScriptableObjects — includes code generation wizards, auto-wiring, and project-wide diagnostics.
PROJECTS.desc: Scriptable System is a Unity workflow package for building event-driven gameplay with ScriptableObjects. It provides reusable Scriptable Variables (SV_*) for shared game state and Scriptable Events (SE_*) for decoupled system communication, replacing hard scene-object references with asset-based messaging. The package ships with editor wizards that generate typed event and variable scripts on demand, context-menu tools that auto-wire listener components and write method stubs directly into your scripts, and custom inspectors that surface every script referencing an event or variable across the entire project. The result is a significantly faster workflow for gameplay programmers and technical designers, with lower coupling, safer refactoring, and stronger inspector visibility in large Unity codebases.
features: Typed Scriptable Events (SE_*) with no-arg and parameterized payloads (int, float, Vector3, custom types)
Shared Scriptable Variables (SV_*) for cross-scene state with built-in primitives and custom type support
3-phase event lifecycle — PreResponse, Response, PostResponse — for ordered side-effect control
Event Wizard: generates SE_* and SE_*Listener scripts with up to 4 typed payload parameters
Variable Wizard: generates SV_* scripts with custom fields and collection wrappers (List, Stack, Queue)
Central manager window (Tools/Raskulls/Scriptables System) for events, variables, and settings
Context-menu listener auto-wiring: attaches listener components and binds matching methods by naming convention
Method stub generation: writes callback stubs into target scripts when no compatible handler exists
Auto Create/Load Assets: scans MonoBehaviour fields and assigns or creates missing ScriptableObject assets
Reference and listener diagnostics: shows every script referencing an event or variable, with broken-binding detection and one-click fix/remove
Play Mode Raise button on event inspectors for quick runtime testing without debug scripts
Configurable naming prefixes and output paths via persistent settings

myWork.title: Core Event & Variable Architecture
myWork.description: Established and evolved the entire runtime foundation — abstract base types (GameEventBase, GameEventListenerBase, ScriptableVariableBase), the register/unregister API, and the typed event ecosystem covering bool, float, int, string, Vector2, Vector3, Transform, GameObject, and custom types.

myWork.title: 3-Phase Event Lifecycle
myWork.description: Gave teams precise ordering control by designing a Pre/Response/Post listener phase model — validation before action, cleanup after — without coupling systems together. Coroutine-backed with an optional OnEventComplete hook.

myWork.title: Event & Variable Wizard (Code Generation)
myWork.description: Eliminated boilerplate by building the Event Wizard and Variable Wizard — generating typed SE_* scripts with up to 4 payload parameters and SV_* scripts with collection wrappers, both validating naming and writing to configurable paths.

myWork.title: Listener Auto-Wiring & Method Stub Generation
myWork.description: Eliminated the most repetitive part of system connection: context-menu tooling that attaches the correct listener component, binds handler methods by naming convention, and writes ready-to-use stubs directly into the target script when none exist.

media.alt: Auto wring context menu showing options to add listeners for compatible events, with a warning when no compatible method is found and an option to generate a method stub in the target script

myWork.title: Reference & Listener Diagnostics
myWork.description: Made large refactors significantly safer by building custom inspectors that surface every script and object referencing an event or variable, detect broken bindings, and offer one-click Fix and Remove actions.

media.alt: Event inspector showing lists of referencing scripts and objects
Show warnings for broken listener bindings and options to fix or remove them

myWork.title: Auto Create/Load Scriptable Assets
myWork.description: Removed repetitive manual asset setup by implementing a context-menu workflow that scans MonoBehaviour fields, finds or creates matching ScriptableObject assets on disk, and assigns them automatically in the inspector.

media.alt: Auto Create/Load context menu showing options to scan the target script for ScriptableObject fields and automatically assign matching assets from disk, with warnings for missing assets and options to create them

myWork.title: Reliability Fixes & Edge Cases
myWork.description: Delivered a sustained series of correctness fixes over the project lifetime: multi-raise deduplication in the same frame, null reference guards in raise and inspector flows, listener method name matching for underscore conventions, abstract class support in asset creation tools, list variable initialization in generated code, and OnEnable double-fire prevention on compile/play cycles.

PROJECTS.name: 10 Tawla
PROJECTS.shortDesc: An online backgammon game with ranked multiplayer, AI opponents, and polished mobile presentation.
PROJECTS.desc: 10 Tawla brings the classic strategy and tension of backgammon to mobile with a polished online experience built for both competitive and casual players. Players can jump into ranked real-time matches, challenge friends, or practice against adaptive AI while enjoying a presentation that captures the feel of a real board through multiple detailed themes and refined interaction. The gameplay loop centers on reading the board, making smart positional choices, and responding to momentum shifts under pressure, while the technical foundation supports matchmaking, ELO progression, social play, and live multiplayer systems that keep every session responsive, fair, and rewarding across iOS and Android.
features: Two unique game modes: Ma7bosa and Thirteen
Online multiplayer with matchmaking
Multiple visual themes and boards
Tournament and ranked play modes
Advanced AI with multiple difficulty levels
Extensive character customization system
Social features and friend challenges
Cross-platform progression

myWork.title: UI/UX Systems & Interface Development
myWork.description: Owned all major UI flows — in-game HUD, main menu, waiting room, profile, settings, game-end, and social panels — including a large-scale prefab restructuring that improved maintainability and added localization for Arabic, French, and Turkish.

myWork.title: Multiplayer & Backend Integration
myWork.description: Maintained real-time multiplayer stability using Nakama — improving disconnect/reconnect handling, matchmaking edge cases, deep link support, and version-check systems, reducing live connection issues post-launch.

myWork.title: Core Gameplay Systems
myWork.description: Hardened gameplay reliability for Tawla and 31 modes by fixing turn flow edge cases, forced moves, rematch logic, and input timing issues — ensuring consistent and fair play across online and offline sessions.

myWork.title: Monetization & Ads Systems
myWork.description: Integrated and expanded monetization systems using AdMob, Unity Ads, and LevelPlay mediation. Implemented and optimized banners, interstitials, and rewarded ads across gameplay and UI flows, ensuring smooth user experience. Managed SDK integrations and platform-specific configurations for Android and iOS.

myWork.title: Performance Optimization & Stability
myWork.description: Improved game performance and reliability through optimization passes and bug fixing, including rendering adjustments (MSAA/anti-aliasing), reduced crashes, and smoother loading behavior. Contributed to analytics integration, data refactoring, and overall system maintainability.

PROJECTS.name: AWAREA App
PROJECTS.shortDesc: AWAREA Mobile application by ZINAD is a gateway to a set of interactive Cybersecurity Awareness content and features that are carefully designed to offer all your Cybersecurity awareness needs in one place from cybersecurity awareness videos, culture-tailored content, interactive cybersecurity awareness games, Cybersecurity news, and more.
PROJECTS.desc:
AWAREA Mobile application by ZINAD is a gateway to a set of interactive Cybersecurity Awareness content and features that are carefully designed to offer all your Cybersecurity awareness needs in one place from cybersecurity awareness videos, culture-tailored content, interactive cybersecurity awareness games, Cybersecurity news, and more.

AWAREA was built around ease of use, access, and conveniently for a smooth experience with fast and easy navigation between the app’s features in both Arabic and English language, delivering the right and necessary information to protect your data and privacy and stay up-to-date with global security threats, trends, and news, for a more secure and stronger organization against breaches, threats, and attacks.

*Login with your ZiSoft Awareness user account where you can access all app features using your ZiSoft Username and Password or via QR Code

*Different access made for Guest Users

Guest Users can use AWAREA and enjoy a glimpse of our Cybersecurity Awareness videos, games, and news.

*Personalize your profile by adding a profile picture

*You can follow ZINAD News, and Cybersecurity News in Arabic or English, Watch Cybersecurity Awareness videos or play ZINAD’s Interactive Awareness Games.

*Customized app theme based on your organization’s branding identity.
features: User & guest login options
Security News Hub (Arabic & English)
Games and videos for cybersecurity awareness
ZINAD News (Arabic and English)
Access to the Awareness Platform (ZiSoft)
Push Notification (Arabic & English)
Profile Personalization

Admin Portal for content management and analytics

Built and refined core app flows including home, settings, profile, notifications, QR login, and game views through extensive prefab and scene iteration, improving usability and localization support.

myWork.title: Firebase Integration & Content Systems
myWork.description: Implemented and maintained Firebase-driven systems for notifications, news, and dynamic content delivery. Developed and improved data flows, handled webview integration and URL routing, and ensured reliable content updates across Arabic and English localized experiences.

Developed dynamic content pages such as news, videos, and tips, with real-time Firebase integration and Unity app bridging.

myWork.title: Security & Data Validation
myWork.description: Improved platform security by implementing XSS sanitization across all dynamic content (news, videos, tips) using DOMPurify and custom URL validation. Defined and maintained Firebase Realtime Database security rules to enforce proper access control for multiple data domains.

myWork.description: Supported Android release pipelines by configuring Addressables, project settings, and platform-specific configurations (Gradle, manifests). Automated Firebase deployment workflows using scripts for hosting, functions, and database rules, improving team efficiency and release reliability.

myWork.description: Integrated and maintained multiple interactive content modules and mini-games (such as phishing awareness, quizzes, and simulations) within the app ecosystem, while contributing to shared systems and reusable components under common modules.

PROJECTS.name: Zinad Security Training Games
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A suite of serious games for cybersecurity awareness training, delivered for enterprise clients across multiple platforms.
PROJECTS.desc:
At Zinad, I worked as a Senior Game Developer on a range of serious games designed to make cybersecurity training more engaging and effective for enterprise users.

My role focused on customizing and evolving existing games to fit different client needs, while also contributing to the development of new features and systems. I worked hands-on across multiple projects—improving stability, refining gameplay, and translating complex security concepts into clear, interactive experiences.

In addition to working on established titles, I played a key role in developing the Emoji Game, where I built several core gameplay systems and features that supported the overall experience.

The projects I contributed to include:
- Phishing Mania – Training players to detect and respond to phishing attacks
- Who Will Be Millionaire – Security Edition – A quiz-based experience for reinforcing cybersecurity knowledge
- The Office VR – An immersive VR simulation focused on workplace security practices
- Password Ninja – A fast-paced game teaching password security fundamentals
- Security Archery VR – A VR experience designed around security awareness and decision-making

Overall, my work at Zinad centered on turning cybersecurity training into something interactive, scalable, and genuinely engaging for users.
features: Multi-platform deployment (Web, VR, Kinect, Android, iOS)
Enterprise-grade security and data handling
Customizable content for different organizations
Engaging gameplay mechanics for learning retention
Accessible across different skill levels
Designed for large-scale employee training programs

myWork.title: Gameplay Systems Development (Emoji Game)
myWork.description:
Played a key role in developing core gameplay systems for the Emoji Game. Designed and implemented a JSON-driven question system, including data structuring and runtime loading to support flexible content updates.

Built and integrated an audio system to manage sound effects and feedback across gameplay. Developed multiple gameplay components including an Emoji Display system triggered by question events, and a Description Panel for dynamic question previews.

Implemented the Slot Board system, which dynamically generates letter slots based on each answer, allowing players to construct words interactively. Added validation logic to evaluate player input, provide real-time feedback, and handle submission flows.

Also developed the main Game Manager responsible for question flow, game state control, and overall gameplay orchestration.

myWork.title: Game Customization & Client Adaptation
myWork.description: Customized and extended existing serious games to meet specific enterprise client requirements. Adapted gameplay logic, content, and user flows to align with different cybersecurity policies and training objectives, ensuring each deployment was relevant and effective.

myWork.title: Maintenance, Optimization & Stability
myWork.description: Maintained and improved existing projects by fixing bugs, optimizing performance, and ensuring smooth user experiences across platforms including WebGL, mobile, VR, and Kinect. Helped keep projects production-ready for enterprise deployment.

myWork.title: Cross-Platform Development
myWork.description: Worked across multiple platforms including Web, VR, Kinect, Android, and iOS. Ensured consistent functionality and performance while adapting input systems and user experience to suit each platform.

myWork.title: Collaboration & Mentorship
myWork.description: Collaborated closely with designers, artists, and other developers, while also supporting and guiding junior developers in their day-to-day tasks. Helped improve code quality and maintain development standards across the team.

PROJECTS.name: Sky Striders
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A fast-paced multiplayer PC game focused on fluid movement, aerial control, and competitive modes.
PROJECTS.desc: Sky Striders is a competitive multiplayer action game built around the thrill of movement mastery, letting players chain together grounded momentum, aerial mobility, and character abilities in fast-paced online matches. Set in a stylized combat arena space where mobility is just as important as attack timing, the experience encourages players to learn traversal, outmaneuver opponents, and exploit verticality to control the flow of each encounter. At its core, the gameplay loop rewards skillful motion, quick decision-making, and coordinated ability use, while the underlying multiplayer architecture and movement systems work together to deliver responsive combat, varied modes, and a strong sense of speed and freedom.
features: Advanced ground & aerial movement
Multiple character classes
Online multiplayer game modes
Dynamic ability-driven gameplay

myWork.title: Multiplayer Networking & Synchronization (Photon Bolt)
myWork.description: Owned the complete Photon Bolt networking layer — player lifecycle, match flow, real-time movement and ability sync, hook mechanics, and game mode state replication — delivering a stable, low-latency competitive experience with robust disconnect handling.

PROJECTS.name: That Just Happened
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A VR safety training game where players prevent workplace accidents using the right equipment.
PROJECTS.desc: That Just Happened is an immersive VR safety experience that places players inside hazardous blue-collar work scenarios where fast thinking and proper preparation can mean the difference between a near miss and a fatal accident. Set in realistic industrial environments, the game asks players to assess unfolding danger, identify what equipment or response is needed, and intervene before co-workers are harmed. The moment-to-moment gameplay is built around presence, urgency, and physical interaction, using VR to make workplace safety lessons more memorable, emotional, and practical while Unreal Engine powers the atmosphere, interaction fidelity, and embodied learning that define the experience.
features: Immersive VR safety simulation
Real-world accident recreation
Interactive safety equipment mechanics
Educational training framework

myWork.title: Endless Mode & AI-Driven Safety Simulation
myWork.description: Designed and implemented an Endless Mode to extend gameplay and reinforce training through repeated dynamic scenarios. Developed worker AI systems that spawn characters with missing safety equipment, including logic for randomization and scenario variation. Built a visual guidance system that displays required safety gear above each worker, enabling players to quickly identify risks and intervene. Focused on creating clear feedback loops and readable interactions to ensure players can prevent accidents effectively, enhancing both gameplay engagement and educational impact.

PROJECTS.name: Skull Switch
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A quick-reflex mobile game about switching lanes to keep skulls safe from incoming threats.
PROJECTS.desc: Skull Switch is a fast-reaction hyper-casual game built around quick reads, lane switching, and constant pressure. Players must protect vulnerable skulls from incoming threats by changing visibility or position at exactly the right moment, creating a tight loop of anticipation, reaction, and recovery that ramps up as patterns become faster and more demanding. The game is designed for mobile-friendly immediacy, with simple controls, sharp visual communication, and a challenge curve focused on reflex mastery. Its appeal comes from how quickly players can understand the mechanic and how hard it becomes to maintain perfect control once the pace intensifies.
features: Reflex-based lane switching
Escalating obstacle patterns
Hyper-casual design

myWork.title: Shop & Economy Systems
myWork.description: Built and stabilized the core shop loop, including loot-box mechanics, skull unlocks, gold/dust economy, and persistent save/load functionality.

myWork.title: UI & Gameplay Flow
myWork.description: Managed UI states and transitions across title, level select, in-game, and result screens, ensuring smooth interactions and clear player feedback.

myWork.title: Level Selection & Generation
myWork.description: Designed normal mode level selector UX and developed editor tooling for procedural level generation, with category-based assignment and optimized content pipelines.

myWork.title: Progression & Rewards
myWork.description: Enhanced result screens, reward presentation, and progression reliability, including save persistence, star tracking, and coin/loot feedback loops.

PROJECTS.name: Prototypes & Demos
PROJECTS.shortDesc: A collection of prototypes and small projects exploring various gameplay concepts, mechanics, and technologies.
PROJECTS.desc: This collection showcases a variety of prototypes and small projects that I developed to explore different gameplay concepts, mechanics, and technologies. Each project served as a testing ground for new ideas, allowing me to experiment with different genres, platforms, and design approaches. From competitive VR multiplayer games to rhythm-based mobile experiences, these prototypes reflect my passion for innovation and my commitment to learning through hands-on development. While not all of these projects were intended for commercial release, they represent important steps in my growth as a game developer and my ongoing exploration of what games can be.
features: Varied gameplay concepts across multiple prototypes
Exploration of different genres and mechanics
Testing ground for new ideas and technologies
Not all projects intended for commercial release
